title: anthemis arenicola var. arenicola
intro: Anthemis arenicola var. arenicola, also known as the sand marigold, is a perennial plant species within the daisy family, native to the Mediterranean region. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The health benefits of Anthemis arenicola var. arenicola include reducing fever, relieving headaches, and alleviating digestive issues, as well as its use as a natural antiseptic and disinfectant. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be made by drying the flowers and leaves and then infusing them in hot water or oil.
